---
title: "1. Server Setup"
description-meta: "Install and start the openme server daemon on Linux — binary install, openme init, and openme serve."
keywords: [openme server setup, SPA server Linux, openme daemon, openme init, openme serve]
---

::: {.callout-note}
The openme server requires **nftables** or **iptables** and is therefore
**Linux-only**. For client-only use on macOS, Windows, iOS or Android see
[Clients →](../clients/index.qmd).
:::

## Install {#install}

### Debian / Ubuntu package (recommended)

Pre-built `.deb` packages are available for all common architectures.

{{< include _install-deb.qmd >}}

::: {.callout-tip}
The `.deb` package installs the binary to `/usr/bin/openme`, creates a
`openme` system user, registers a systemd service, and starts it automatically.
You can skip the **Start the Server** section below and go straight to
[Add a client →](adding-clients.qmd).
:::

### Pre-built binary

{{< include _install-binary.qmd >}}

### From source

```bash
git clone https://github.com/merlos/openme
cd openme/cli
go mod download
go build -o openme ./cmd/openme
sudo mv openme /usr/local/bin/
```

---

## Initialise {#init}

`openme init` generates a fresh Curve25519 keypair and writes the server
configuration to `/etc/openme/config.yaml`.

```bash
sudo openme init --server myserver.example.com
```

| Flag | Default | Description |
|------|---------|-------------|
| `--server` | *(required)* | Public hostname or IP — used in generated client configs |
| `--port` | `54154` | UDP knock port and TCP health port |
| `--firewall` | `nft` | Firewall backend: `nft` or `iptables` |
| `--force` | `false` | Overwrite an existing config |

The command prints the server's Curve25519 public key.
`openme add` uses it automatically when provisioning clients.

---

## Start the Server {#serve}

```bash
sudo openme serve
```

For production, enable the systemd service (the `.deb` package does this
automatically):

```bash
sudo systemctl enable --now openme
sudo systemctl status openme
```

Full systemd unit reference and hardening options:
[Server → Service](../server/service.qmd).

---

Next: [Add a client →](adding-clients.qmd)
